description:
Similar to the previous issue regarding PR maintainer issues: When `prBaseBranch` is set to `dev`, protoLabs Studio creates PRs targeting `dev` but does not verify that the repository's GitHub Actions workflows are configured to trigger on that branch — in our case all 4 workflows (build, test, format, security) only had `branches: [main]`, so CI checks never ran on any of the 10 audit PRs, the system's `check_pr_status` reported them as "1 pending" indefinitely, and after just 9 minutes of no PR activity each feature auto-transitioned from `review` to `blocked` with no actionable error message; compounding this, CodeRabbit was rate-limited by the simultaneous batch of 10 PRs and set its commit status to `FAILURE`, which the system treated as a real check failure rather than a transient condition, and all 10 PRs lacked ownership watermarks (`instanceId: null`) so the PR Maintainer crew skipped them entirely during its 10-minute sweep — the net result was 10 features silently stuck for 5+ hours, $15+ in wasted agent budget, and required direct Ava intervention to diagnose, fix the workflow files (PR #161), resolve CodeRabbit threads, rebase conflicting branches, and manually merge all 12 PRs into `dev`. 

Please only take these as possible solutions. I'm a horrible coder, and the team should REALLY get to the bottom of the issue in case I'm wrong. 🙂 

**Four potential issues need review from the protoLabs team:** (1) validate CI workflow triggers match `prBaseBranch` during `setuplab` and on settings change, (2) increase the review-to-blocked timeout from 9 minutes to a configurable 30-60 minutes with exponential backoff, (3) detect CodeRabbit rate-limit `FAILURE` as transient and retry rather than treating it as terminal, and (4) ensure PR ownership watermarks are always injected so the crew never silently skips managed PRs.

descriptionHistory.description:
Similar to the previous issue regarding PR maintainer issues: When `prBaseBranch` is set to `dev`, protoLabs Studio creates PRs targeting `dev` but does not verify that the repository's GitHub Actions workflows are configured to trigger on that branch — in our case all 4 workflows (build, test, format, security) only had `branches: [main]`, so CI checks never ran on any of the 10 audit PRs, the system's `check_pr_status` reported them as "1 pending" indefinitely, and after just 9 minutes of no PR activity each feature auto-transitioned from `review` to `blocked` with no actionable error message; compounding this, CodeRabbit was rate-limited by the simultaneous batch of 10 PRs and set its commit status to `FAILURE`, which the system treated as a real check failure rather than a transient condition, and all 10 PRs lacked ownership watermarks (`instanceId: null`) so the PR Maintainer crew skipped them entirely during its 10-minute sweep — the net result was 10 features silently stuck for 5+ hours, $15+ in wasted agent budget, and required direct Ava intervention to diagnose, fix the workflow files (PR #161), resolve CodeRabbit threads, rebase conflicting branches, and manually merge all 12 PRs into `dev`. 

Please only take these as possible solutions. I'm a horrible coder, and the team should REALLY get to the bottom of the issue in case I'm wrong. 🙂 

**Four potential issues need review from the protoLabs team:** (1) validate CI workflow triggers match `prBaseBranch` during `setuplab` and on settings change, (2) increase the review-to-blocked timeout from 9 minutes to a configurable 30-60 minutes with exponential backoff, (3) detect CodeRabbit rate-limit `FAILURE` as transient and retry rather than treating it as terminal, and (4) ensure PR ownership watermarks are always injected so the crew never silently skips managed PRs.
